name: python3-typing-extensions
title: python3-typing-extensions
summary: Backported and Experimental Type Hints for Python (Python3 version)
description:
Backported and Experimental Type Hints for Python (Python3 version)
The "typing_extensions" module serves two related purposes:
.
1. Enable use of new type system features on older Python versions.
2. Enable experimentation with new type system PEPs before they are accepted
and added to the "typing" module.
.
"typing_extensions" is treated specially by static type checkers such as mypy
and pyright. Objects defined in "typing_extensions" are treated the same
way as equivalent forms in "typing".
.
This package contains the Python 3 version of the library.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-typing-extensions because Backported and Experimental Type Hints for Python (Python3 version).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-typing-extensions as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-typing-extensions with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-typing-extensions --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
